title: [Feature] An option for a splash screen would be nice
author: ethanrushh
body: I believe this isn't possible on the user side without modifying the flutter-pi embedder directly. Ideally, it would be nice to be able to have a splash screen while the flutter app initializes and the first frame is drawn. I think it would make sense to have this available as an argument when running `flutter-pi <project>` (e.g. `flutter-pi --splash example-splash.png <project>`).

title: audioplayers plugin seems partially broken
author: ethanrushh
body:
The audioplayers plugin seems half-baked. I was able to get it working and audio output does work, though version 5.0.0 is quite outdated at this point and any other version flat out will not work in my testing. player.onPositionChanged never seems to be fired, nor can I get positional updates via player.eventStream (listening for AudioEventType.position). The exact same set of code does fire when using the official linux embedder on the same hardware and on other hardware. I also frequently get a non-implementation error for 'method cancel'. 

Is there a recommended workaround for this? I was unable to find other issues that mention this specific problem or any documentation suggesting its intentionally not a supported feature.

title: Flutter-pi issues on Raspberry Pi 5
author: des256
body:
Hi, flutter-pi has a few issues on Raspberry Pi 5 that become apparent with more recent linux kernels. Later `vc4-kms-dsi` overlay support very simple and more limited hardware planes. I found the planes would not support hardware rotation at all, and the available pixel formats are also limited. For our project (github.com/mind-children) I forked flutter-pi (github.com/des256/flutter-pi) and commented out some of the rotation checks to make it functional again. Actual rotation now happens in the flutter app with a `RotatedBox()`. Also, I got the output to work with `--pixelformat RGB565`, which translates internally to RG16, which is supported by the hardware plane.

@ardera I did not design any proper PR for your repo yet, because I'm not sure how you would want to solve this going forward (i.e. rotation not being available on basic `vc4-kms-dsi`).

title: Add support for flutter_webrtc plugin (GLib main context incompatibility)
author: iagormoraes
body:
## Summary

The `flutter_webrtc` plugin does not work with flutter-pi due to a main loop incompatibility. The plugin's Linux implementation uses GLib's `g_main_context_invoke()` for thread marshalling, but flutter-pi uses libsystemd's sd-event loop instead.

## Environment

- **Device**: Raspberry Pi 5
- **flutter-pi version**: latest from source
- **flutter_webrtc version**: 0.12.12
- **Flutter SDK**: 3.10.7

## Error Output

```
[ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(40)] Unhandled Exception: Call initialize before setting the stream
#0      RTCVideoRenderer.srcObject= (package:flutter_webrtc/src/native/rtc_video_renderer_impl.dart:61)
#1      WebRtc._cleanup (package:frame_client/features/agent/providers/webrtc_provider.dart:395)
<asynchronous suspension>

[ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(40)] Unhandled Exception: WebSocketChannelException: SocketException: Connection refused (OS Error: Connection refused, errno = 111), address = localhost, port = 37130

[ERROR:flutter/runtime/dart_vm_initializer.cc(40)] Unhandled Exception: WebSocketChannelException: SocketException: Connection refused (OS Error: Connection refused, errno = 111), address = localhost, port = 42876
```

The random high ports (37130, 42876, etc.) suggest libwebrtc's internal networking fails during ICE candidate gathering because initialization callbacks never complete.

## Root Cause Analysis

The `flutter_webrtc` plugin's Linux implementation relies on GLib for thread marshalling in [`task_runner_linux.cc`](https://github.com/flutter-webrtc/flutter-webrtc/blob/main/linux/task_runner_linux.cc):

```cpp
void TaskRunnerLinux::EnqueueTask(TaskClosure task) {
  // ...
  GMainContext* context = g_main_context_default();
  if (context) {
    g_main_context_invoke(context, /* callback */, this);
  }
}
```

This expects GLib's `GMainLoop` to be running and pumping the default main context. However, flutter-pi uses **libsystemd's sd-event** for its event loop (as mentioned in the README: "libsystemd provides the event loop and dbus support for flutter-pi").

Since `g_main_context_invoke()` schedules work on a GLib main context that flutter-pi never iterates, the WebRTC callbacks are never executed. This causes:

1. Initialization sequences to hang or fail silently
2. Native resources to remain uninitialized
3. Subsequent operations to fail with confusing errors

## Comparison with flutter-elinux

The `flutter_webrtc` plugin works with [flutter-elinux](https://github.com/sony/flutter-elinux) after thread marshalling fixes were implemented (see [flutter-elinux-plugins#7](https://github.com/sony/flutter-elinux-plugins/issues/7)). The flutter-elinux project appears to use GLib's main loop, which is why `g_main_context_invoke()` works there.

## Possible Solutions

1. **flutter-pi side**: Add GLib main context integration alongside sd-event, or provide an API for plugins to schedule work on the main thread

2. **flutter_webrtc side**: Create an alternative `task_runner` implementation using sd-event for flutter-pi compatibility

3. **Hybrid approach**: flutter-pi could expose a platform-agnostic callback mechanism that plugins can use for thread marshalling

## Related Issues/References

- [flutter-elinux-plugins#7 - WebRTC plugin support](https://github.com/sony/flutter-elinux-plugins/issues/7) - Documents the thread marshalling fix needed for embedded Linux
- [webrtc-sdk/libwebrtc](https://github.com/webrtc-sdk/libwebrtc) - The C++ wrapper used by flutter_webrtc for desktop/Linux/embedded

title: Couldn't import dmabuf as vulkan device memory. vkAllocateMemory: VK_ERROR_INVALID_EXTERNAL_HANDLE
author: rayui
body:
Hi. I'm building my app on arm64 with vulkan on rpi4 raspbian trixie.

`Linux scubaru 6.12.47+rpt-rpi-v8 #1 SMP PREEMPT Debian 1:6.12.47-1+rpt1 (2025-09-16) aarch64 GNU/Linux`

I'm building using the following command:

`flutterpi_tool build --release --arch=arm64 --cpu=pi4`

My flutter env setup is:

```
Flutter 3.38.5 • channel [user-branch] • unknown source
Framework • revision f6ff1529fd (4 weeks ago) • 2025-12-11 11:50:07 -0500
Engine • hash c108a94d7a8273e112339e6c6833daa06e723a54 (revision 1527ae0ec5) (24 days ago) • 2025-12-11 15:04:31.000Z
Tools • Dart 3.10.4 • DevTools 2.51.1
```

This is a stable release from source tag.

The app builds fine in both flutter-pi and flutter. It runs fine on x64 and arm64 without Vulkan. When I run it on arm64 with Vulkan enabled (compiled on arm64) I get the following:

```
/usr/local/bin/flutter-pi --vulkan --videomode=1920x1080@60 --release .
vk_gbm_render_surface.c: Couldn't import dmabuf as vulkan device memory. vkAllocateMemory: VK_ERROR_INVALID_EXTERNAL_HANDLE
vk_gbm_render_surface.c: Could not initialize vulkan GBM framebuffer.
window.c: Couldn't create Vulkan GBM rendering surface.
compositor_ng.c: Couldn't create render surface for flutter to render into.
[ERROR:flutter/shell/platform/embedder/embedder.cc(1393)] Could not create the embedder backing store.
```

From what I understand of the flutter-pi source code, this is quite late on in the process, after the memory has been allocated for DMA. I have the DRM modules installed in config.txt. No overclocking or gpu mem specified.

Any advice would be gratefully received.

title: Flutter-pi on Raspberry Pi 4B with 3.5" SPI TFT (fbdev) – how to make it work?
author: nikolaihen
body:
Hi, I’m new to Linux and embedded systems and I’m trying to run a Flutter app on a Raspberry Pi 4B (4GB RAM) using this 3.5" SPI display:  
[https://www.lcdwiki.com/3.5inch_RPi_Display](https://www.lcdwiki.com/3.5inch_RPi_Display)

I have successfully installed the LCD35-show drivers from:  
[https://github.com/goodtft/LCD-show](https://github.com/goodtft/LCD-show)

However, as far as I understand, the display currently uses fbdev (framebuffer) and not KMS/DRM, which prevents `flutter-pi` from working out-of-the-box.

---

## System Info
```
OS: Linux raspberrypi 6.12.47+rpt-rpi-v8 #1 SMP PREEMPT Debian 1:6.12.47-1+rpt1 (2025-09-16) aarch64 GNU/Linux
Flutter (host Windows machine): 3.35.1
Flutter-pi build command (host Windows machine): flutterpi_tool build --arch=arm64 --cpu=pi4 --release
```

## Symptoms / Errors
- Running `flutter-pi --release ~/app` outputs:
```
window.c: Could not find a preferred output mode!
flutter-pi.c: Couldn't create KMS window.
```

- In `raspi-config`:
  - I have no "GL Driver" under "Advanced Options"
    - But have added `dtoverlay=vc4-fkms-v3d` to /boot/config.txt
  - I have no "GPU Memory" under "Performance Options"

---

## Current `/boot/config.txt`
```text
dtoverlay=vc4-fkms-v3d
max_framebuffers=2
disable_fw_kms_setup=1
arm_64bit=1
disable_overscan=1
arm_boost=1
hdmi_force_hotplug=1
dtparam=i2c_arm=on
dtparam=spi=on
enable_uart=1
dtoverlay=tft35a:rotate=90
hdmi_group=2
hdmi_mode=1
hdmi_mode=87
hdmi_cvt 480 320 60 6 0 0 0
hdmi_drive=2
```

## Devices / Drivers
```
$ ls /dev/dri/
by-path  card0  card1  renderD128
```

```
$ ls /dev/fb*
/dev/fb0
```

- Display is currently using fbdev via /dev/fb0 (fb_ili9486 driver)
  - frame buffer, 480x320, 300 KiB video memory, 32 KiB buffer memory, fps=31, spi0.0 at 16 MHz
- DRM driver for ILI9486 exists on my system:
/lib/modules/6.12.47+rpt-rpi-v8/kernel/drivers/gpu/drm/tiny/ili9486.ko.xz
- I’m unsure how to properly load/use this DRM driver instead of fbdev.

## What I want
- Run flutter-pi on this SPI display, even if performance isn’t perfect.
- Clarification on whether:
  1. I can make flutter-pi work with fbdev (any existing workaround/patch?), or
  2. I should attempt using the ILI9486 DRM driver, and how to do that properly.

Any guidance from people who have run flutter-pi on a similar TFT or SPI framebuffer would be hugely appreciated.

title: [Beginner] unable to run app built with flutterpi-tool on x86 linux on RPi4
author: tadelv
body:
Hello all,

I am trying to use Github actions to build my app using flutter-pi. 
I am able to install flutter-pi tool and built the assets with it, but when I transfer that folder to my RPi and try to run it with flutter-pi, I get the following message:
```
$ flutter-pi --release rea-assets/
filesystem_layout.c: app elf file "/home/pi/rea-assets/app.so" does not exist, but is necessary for release/profile mode.
```

Here's my github actions file contents:

```
  build-raspberrypi:
    name: Build for Raspberry Pi (ARM64 / Pi 4+)
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04     # ARM64 hosted runner label
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Install GTK+ 3.0
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y libgtk-3-dev

      - name: Set up Flutter
        uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2
        with:
          channel: stable
          flutter-version: 3.35.4
      
      - name: Run git safe dir
        run: git config --global --add safe.directory /opt/hostedtoolcache/flutter/${{ steps.flutter-action.outputs.CHANNEL }}-${{ steps.flutter-action.outputs.VERSION }}-${{ steps.flutter-action.outputs.ARCHITECTURE }}

      - name: Add Flutter-Pi
        run: flutter pub global activate flutterpi_tool

      - name: build bundle
        run: flutter build bundle --release --target-platform linux-arm64

      - name: list
        run: ls -l

      # - name: Install dependencies
      #   run: flutter pub get

      - name: Build Linux app (ARM64)
        run: flutterpi_tool build --arch=arm64 --cpu=pi4 --release 

      - name: Upload Raspberry Pi build (ARM64)
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: raspberrypi-linux-arm64-app
          path: build/flutter_assets
          include-hidden-files: true

```

I assume I'm doing something wrong at the "build bundle" (and all subsequent) step(s)?

title: Display Scaling Issue on Raspberry Pi 5 with MIPI Display
author: mosynthkey
body:
First of all, thank you for this wonderful project! I'm currently developing a Flutter application that runs on macOS, Windows, and Raspberry Pi 5, and I'm having a great development experience.

I have a question regarding screen size and DPR (Device Pixel Ratio) handling.
On macOS and Windows, widgets are displayed at the expected Flutter widget sizes. However, when I tested on Raspberry Pi 5 with a MIPI display, the DPR is reported as 2.24, causing widgets to appear much larger than intended.
(I'm getting the DPR using mediaQuery.devicePixelRatio)

After examining the source code, I found that the pixel ratio is calculated based on the actual display size using:
```
pixel_ratio = (10.0 * width) / (width_mm * 38.0);
```
Even when I pass the actual screen size using the -d option, I get the same result (DPR of 2.24 with oversized widget display).

What would be the best approach to resolve this issue of oversized widgets?
- Modify flutter_pi to add an option that forces pixel_ratio = 1
- Use Transform on the Flutter app side to scale to the ideal size
- Provide a fake larger display size via the -d option to achieve the ideal widget size

Additionally, could you help me understand what the value 38.0 in the pixel_ratio calculation represents?

Thank you for taking the time to help with this issue.

title: flutter-pi on NXP i.MX 8QM with Vivante GC7000
author: ponymorphism
body:
Hi,

we are trying to get flutter-pi running on our embedded platform using the i.MX 8QM processor with a Vivante GC7000 rev 6009 GPU.
I'm building the system image using buildroot and got Mesa 3D with Etnaviv drivers running. Kmscube runs just fine both with and without atomic modesetting.

Running our flutter application I get errors like this:

modesetting.c: Could not commit display update. drmModeAtomicCommit: Invalid argument
window.c: Could not commit frame request.

and the display doesn't change.

I traced this so far as I suspect the buffer modifers "VIVANTE_TILED(0x600000000000001)" and "VIVANTE_SUPER_TILED(0x600000000000002)" passed to egl_gbm_render_surface_new_with_egl_config in window.c.
If I change count_modifiers_for_pixel_format and extract_modifiers_for_pixel_format in such a way that VIVANTE_SUPER_TILED is ignored, I get an output but with some artifacts. If I filter both, the application seems to run fine.

A quick test showed that the cursor doesn't work properly and I get "modesetting.c: Couldn't move mouse cursor. drmModeMoveCursor: Bad address" errors, but I didn't investigate there so far, as our application is mostly touch based.

I realize that my filtering is probably just a quick kludge, so what would be an appropriate way to handle this and how could I proceed?

Thank you so much for this great software!
